title: Animated GUI
description: Smooth, fully customizable animations for Minecraft's instant GUI: sliding menus, gliding item slots, smooth creative and list scrolling, and a flat settings screen. Client-side; the vanilla look is untouched.
body:
# Animated GUI

Animated GUI replaces Minecraft's instant, snappy interface transitions with smooth, fully customizable animations — **without changing how any vanilla screen looks.** Menus fade and slide, item stacks glide between slots, the creative menu scrolls by the pixel, and the hotbar selector slides instead of teleporting. Every animation has its own style, speed and easing curve, all tuned from a clean, flat settings screen.

## What it animates

- **Chat** — new messages slide up smoothly as they arrive instead of popping in.
- **Item slots** — picked-up and moved stacks glide between slots with a configurable easing curve.
- **Creative inventory** — pixel-smooth scrolling instead of jumping a whole row at a time.
- **Hotbar selector** — slides between slots as you switch.
- **Inventory & pause / menu screens** — fade and scale on open and close, each with its own style and speed.
- **Menu navigation** — pick how moving between screens animates: Slide, Zoom, Fade or Swap.
- **Smooth list scrolling** — every vanilla-list screen (Options, Mod Menu, config screens and any mod that reuses the vanilla list) scrolls eased instead of snapping. No mod is hardcoded — it just works.

## Fully customizable

Open the settings from Mod Menu to reach a flat, Sodium-style tabbed screen (General / Inventory / Game menu / Chat). Every feature has its own toggle, duration and easing curve — Linear, Smooth, Ease-out, Overshoot, Elastic, Bounce and more. A single master switch instantly freezes everything back to vanilla.

## Good to know

- This is a **client-side** mod. It changes only your own interface and is not required on servers.
- It does **not** restyle vanilla GUIs; it only animates their motion. Textures and layouts stay exactly as Minecraft draws them.
- Only the mod's **own** settings screen uses the custom flat style.

## Requirements

- Minecraft **26.1.2**
- **Fabric Loader** 0.19.2 or newer
- **Fabric API**
- **Mod Menu** (optional, to open the settings screen)

Licensed under MIT.
